items.title: Young Australians reject budget as One Nation popularity climbs further

children.content: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they'd rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

items.title: Australia scraps new Virginia-class submarines in AUKUS shake-up

children.content: The US, UK and Australia have announced they will "streamline" the purchase, with Australia no longer buying new submarines, and acquiring three second-hand ones.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Netanyahu orders deeper incursion into Lebanon as Israeli troops seize castle

children.content: Israeli troops have captured a strategic mountain topped with a Crusader-built castle in southern Lebanon, in their deepest incursion into the country in decades.

image.altText: Three soldiers stand looking at an old crumbling structure in the near distance

items.title: Barnaby Joyce's link to how e-bikes came to be everywhere

items.title: Lucky escape for pair as tree falls on car amid WA wild weather

children.content: Two people have had a lucky escape after a tree fell onto their car on a major highway in WA's South West this afternoon, as strong winds lashed the region.

items.title: Horses bolt through Rome after police officer sets off fireworks

children.content: Fireworks set off without permission by a police officer during a rehearsal for Italy's Republic Day parade startled dozens of horses, sending them bolting through the capital and injuring several riders.

image.altText: A white horse with full riding gear but no rider runs down a paved street at night.

items.title: Divers at Laos cave search for two men thought to be trapped further down

items.title: The combo that makes tonight's full moon a 'last in a generation' occurrence

items.title: At 15, Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27, he’s making history

items.title: Police beat a woman in a Sydney street. Their own camera captured their cruelty

items.title: Iran's internet revival unearths videos regime 'didn't want world to witness'

items.title: Socceroos defeated by Mexico despite encouraging second-half showing

items.title: Why are Australians still getting lost and dying in the outback?

items.title: Applications to protect Victoria Park rejected ahead of construction of Olympic stadium

children.content: The federal government has rejected two applications for protection of Brisbane's Victoria Park ahead of construction on the Olympic stadium beginning tomorrow, but is still considering another five.

items.title: As One Nation broadens its economic appeal, the Liberals return to culture wars

children.content: The Coalition's answer to the rise of conservative populism has been to treat it primarily as a cultural phenomenon. Under Angus Taylor, that instinct appears to be hardening.

items.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?

items.title: Police urge community caution as man remains at large after Melbourne double stabbing

items.title: Police 'open' to learning from volunteers who found remains in renewed search

items.title: 'Losing a generation': Why some countries are moving fast on AI in schools

items.title: This preacher was once part of NZ's gang world. Now he's trying to heal it

items.title: AUKUS partners unveil plan to develop underwater drones

items.title: How Tiananmen Square vigils in Hong Kong have become unlawful
